summary: Gruber analyzes what Apple will likely ship in the box with the rumored headphone-jack-free iPhone, after a leaked video shows a Lightning-to-3.5mm adapter. He lays out five possible bundling options and argues strongly that Apple should ship wireless ear buds, not Lightning ear buds or the old 3.5mm pair with an adapter. His reasoning centers on message consistency: if Apple removes the jack, bundling an adapter contradicts the statement that most people don't need it. He also notes that Lightning ear buds would create a compatibility problem with Macs, while wireless would work across devices. He speculates that a new wireless protocol could explain delayed MacBook Pro updates.
key_points: Shipping a 3.5mm adapter in the box would contradict Apple's message that the headphone jack is no longer necessary
Bundling old 3.5mm EarPods with a dongle would make the included headphone experience worse than previous iPhones
Lightning ear buds create a new compatibility problem since Macs don't have Lightning ports
The move should be about transitioning from wired to wireless, not from one wired connector to another
A proprietary wireless protocol (not necessarily Bluetooth) could explain the delayed MacBook Pro refresh
money_quotes: When Apple eliminates ports, they tend to do so in favor of wireless technology.
If they ship it in the box, the implied message is that a 3.5mm headphone port is something every iPhone should have, which is contrary to the decision to remove the port from the device itself.
If they're eliminating the port, they're saying most people should not need it.
This would make using the included EarPods worse on the new iPhones than it was on older iPhones.
Note that I keep saying wireless ear buds, not Bluetooth. It could be Bluetooth, but doesn't have to be.
This move should be about moving away from wired headphones period, not moving from one wired jack to another.
The best way to achieve that is for Apple to ship wireless ear buds in the box.
key_insight: Removing the headphone jack only makes sense as a statement if Apple fully commits to wireless by shipping wireless ear buds in the box, rather than just swapping one wired connector for another.
